action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|#2.......|
 2|#...p....|
 3|1......#.|
 4|##....#..|
 5|......A..|
 6|......#B#|
 7|..#.#.#.#|
 8|.q.yx##..|
 9|S....##..|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (1,9) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (1,9), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (7,5): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (8,6): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {0, 3}. So it is open at t=0,3,4,7,... and closed at t=1,2,5,6,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (5,2); t mod 4=1: (6,2); t mod 4=2: (7,2); t mod 4=3: (6,2). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (2,8); t mod 6=1: (1,8); t mod 6=2: (1,7); t mod 6=3: (2,7); t mod 6=4: (1,7); t mod 6=5: (1,8). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (1,3) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (5,8) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (2,1) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (4,8) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 45 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 9 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive only a per-tick log of OK/BUMP/CAUGHT/PICKED UP/DELIVERED. You are NEVER told where you are or why you bumped - track your position yourself.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I start at (1,9) at t=0. My goal is to pick up Parcel 1 at (1,3) and Parcel 2 at (2,1), then deliver them to (5,8) and (4,8) respectively.
First, I need to navigate to the pickup locations.
Guard Q is at (2,8) at t=0, moving to (1,8) at t=1, (1,7) at t=2, (2,7) at t=3, (1,7) at t=4, (1,8) at t=5.
Guard P is at (5,2) at t=0, moving to (6,2) at t=1, (7,2) at t=2, (6,2) at t=3.

Path to Parcel 1 (1,3):
Current: (1,9).
t=0 -> t=1: Move N to (1,8). Guard Q moves to (1,8) at t=1? No, Q moves *after* my move.
Let's re-verify the order:
1. My move resolves (arrive at t+1).
2. Guards move to t+1 position.
3. Check collision.

At t=0, I am at (1,9).
Move N: Arrive (1,8) at t=1.
Guard Q at t=1 is at (1,8).
Collision! I cannot move N at t=0.
Move E: Arrive (2,9) at t=1. Guard Q is at (1,8). Safe.
Move S: Wall.
Move W: Wall.
Wait: Stay (1,9). Guard Q moves to (1,8). Safe.

Let's try to go around Q.
Option A: Wait at t=0.
t=0: WAIT. Pos (1,9). Q moves to (1,8).
t=1: Move N to (1,8). Arrive t=2.
Guard Q at t=2 is at (1,7).
Collision check: I am at (1,8), Q is at (1,7). Safe.
t=2: Move N to (1,7). Arrive t=3.
Guard Q at t=3 is at (2,7).
Collision check: I am at (1,7), Q is at (2,7). Safe.
t=3: Move N to (1,6). Arrive t=4.
Guard Q at t=4 is at (1,7).
Collision check: I am at (1,6), Q is at (1,7). Safe.
t=4: Move N to (1,5). Arrive t=5.
Guard Q at t=5 is at (1,8).
Collision check: I am at (1,5), Q is at (1,8). Safe.
t=5: Move N to (1,4). Arrive t=6.
Guard Q at t=6 (0 mod 6) is at (2,8).
Collision check: Safe.
t=6: Move N to (1,3). Arrive t=7.
Pick up Parcel 1.

Let's check the first 5 moves of this plan: WAIT, N, N, N, N.
t=0: WAIT. Pos (1,9).
t=1: N -> (1,8). Q at (1,8)? No, Q moves *after* I arrive.
Wait, the rule says: "If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT."
Sequence:
1. I move to (1,8) at t=1.
2. Guard Q moves to its t=1 position: (1,8).
3. Check collision: I am at (1,8), Q is at (1,8). CAUGHT.
So moving N at t=0 is bad.
Moving N at t=1 (after waiting):
t=0: WAIT. Pos (1,9). Q moves to (1,8).
t=1: Move N. Arrive (1,8) at t=2.
Guard Q moves to t=2 position: (1,7).
Collision: I (1,8) vs Q (1,7). Safe.
t=2: Move N. Arrive (1,7) at t=3.
Guard Q moves to t=3 position: (2,7).
Collision: I (1,7) vs Q (2,7). Safe.
t=3: Move N. Arrive (1,6) at t=4.
Guard Q moves to t=4 position: (1,7).
Collision: I (1,6) vs Q (1,7). Safe.
t=4: Move N. Arrive (1,5) at t=5.
Guard Q moves to t=5 position: (1,8).
Collision: I (1,5) vs Q (1,8). Safe.
t=5: Move N. Arrive (1,4) at t=6.
Guard Q moves to t=6 position: (2,8).
Collision: Safe.

So the plan WAIT, N, N, N, N gets me to (1,4) at t=5.
Next moves would be N to (1,3) at t=6 (Pickup).
Let's verify the path to (1,3) is clear of walls.
(1,9) -> (1,8) -> (1,7) -> (1,6) -> (1,5) -> (1,4) -> (1,3).
Map check:
Row 9: S.... (1,9 is S)
Row 8: .q.yx (1,8 is .)
Row 7: ..#.# (1,7 is .)
Row 6: ...... (1,6 is .)
Row 5: ...... (1,5 is .)
Row 4: ##.... (1,4 is #? Wait. Row 4 is `##....#..`. Col 1 is #, Col 2 is #.
Ah, (1,4) is a wall!
Let's re-read the map.
Row 4: `##....#..`
Col 1: #
Col 2: #
Col 3: .
So (1,4) is a wall. I cannot go straight up column 1 past row 5.
I need to go around the wall at (1,4) and (2,4).
The wall is at (1,4) and (2,4).
So I must be at (3,4) or higher to pass?
Let's look at the map again.
Row 4: `##....#..` -> (1,4)=#, (2,4)=#, (3,4)=., (4,4)=., (5,4)=., (6,4)=., (7,4)=#, (8,4)=., (9,4)=.
Row 3: `1......#.` -> (1,3)=1, (2,3)=., (3,3)=., (4,3)=., (5,3)=., (6,3)=., (7,3)=., (8,3)=#, (9,3)=.
Row 5: `......A..` -> (1,5)=., (2,5)=., (3,5)=., (4,5)=., (5,5)=., (6,5)=., (7,5)=A, (8,5)=., (9,5)=.

So to get from (1,5) to (1,3), I cannot go (1,5)->(1,4) because (1,4) is a wall.
I must go (1,5) -> (2,5) -> (3,5) -> (3,4) -> (3,3) -> (2,3) -> (1,3).
Or (1,5) -> (2,5) -> (2,4) is wall.
So path: (1,5) -> (2,5) -> (3,5) -> (3,4) -> (3,3) -> (2,3) -> (1,3).
Let's re-evaluate the first 5 moves.
Start (1,9).
t=0: WAIT. (1,9). Q->(1,8).
t=1: N -> (1,8). Q->(1,7). Safe.
t=2: N -> (1,7). Q->(2,7). Safe.
t=3: N -> (1,6). Q->(1,7). Safe.
t=4: N -> (1,5). Q->(1,8). Safe.
t=5: E -> (2,5). Q->(2,8). Safe.
t=6: E -> (3,5). Q->(1,8). Safe.
t=7: N -> (3,4). Q->(1,7). Safe.
t=8: N -> (3,3). Q->(2,7). Safe.
t=9: W -> (2,3). Q->(1,7). Safe.
t=10: W -> (1,3). Pickup Parcel 1.

So the first 5 moves should be: WAIT, N, N, N, N.
This gets me to (1,5) at t=5.
Wait, if I do WAIT, N, N, N, N:
t=0: WAIT. Pos (1,9).
t=1: N. Pos (1,8).
t=2: N. Pos (1,7).
t=3: N. Pos (1,6).
t=4: N. Pos (1,5).
t=5: N? No, (1,4) is wall.
So the 5th move in the plan must be E to (2,5).
Let's re-calculate the 5 moves.
Move 1 (t=0->1): WAIT. Pos (1,9).
Move 2 (t=1->2): N. Pos (1,8).
Move 3 (t=2->3): N. Pos (1,7).
Move 4 (t=3->4): N. Pos (1,6).
Move 5 (t=4->5): N. Pos (1,5).
Wait, if I do 4 Ns, I am at (1,5) at t=4?
Start t=0 at (1,9).
1. WAIT -> t=1 at (1,9).
2. N -> t=2 at (1,8).
3. N -> t=3 at (1,7).
4. N -> t=4 at (1,6).
5. N -> t=5 at (1,5).
Yes.
So the plan WAIT, N, N, N, N puts me at (1,5) at t=5.
Is (1,5) safe?
At t=5, Q is at (1,8). I am at (1,5). Safe.
Next step (t=5->6) I need to go E to (2,5).
But I only submit 5 moves.
So the plan is WAIT, N, N, N, N.
Position after plan: (1,5).

Let's double check Guard Q positions.
t=0: (2,8)
t=1: (1,8)
t=2: (1,7)
t=3: (2,7)
t=4: (1,7)
t=5: (1,8)
t=6: (2,8)

My path:
t=0: (1,9)
t=1: (1,9) [WAIT] -> Q moves to (1,8). Safe.
t=2: (1,8) [N] -> Q moves to (1,7). Safe.
t=3: (1,7) [N] -> Q moves to (2,7). Safe.
t=4: (1,6) [N] -> Q moves to (1,7). Safe.
t=5: (1,5) [N] -> Q moves to (1,8). Safe.

Looks good.

POSITION: (1,5)
PLAN: WAIT N N N N

Now I need to deliver Parcel 1 to (5,8).
Path from (1,3) to (5,8):
(1,3) -> (2,3) -> (3,3) -> (4,3) -> (5,3) -> (5,4) -> (5,5) -> (5,6) -> (5,7) -> (5,8).

Check Row 7: (4,7) is open (Col 4 is .).
Check Row 8: (4,8) is 'y' (open).
This works.
So path: (1,3) -> (2,3) -> (3,3) -> (4,3) -> (5,3) -> (5,4) -> (5,5) -> (5,6) -> (4,6) -> (4,7) -> (4,8).
Wait, I need to deliver to (5,8) for Parcel 1.

Col 1: .
Col 2: q
Col 3: .

Col 5: x

Col 7: #

So (5,8) is 'x'.
How to get to (5,8)?
From (4,8) (which is 'y'), I can go E to (5,8).
So path: ... -> (4,7) -> (4,8) -> (5,8).
Let's re-verify the path from (1,3) to (5,8).
(1,3) -> (2,3) -> (3,3) -> (4,3) -> (5,3) -> (5,4) -> (5,5) -> (5,6) -> (4,6) -> (4,7) -> (4,8) -> (5,8).

(4,7) to (4,8): Row 8 `.q.yx##..`. (4,8) is 'y' (open).
(4,8) to (5,8): Row 8. (5,8) is 'x' (open).
This path works.

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am currently at (4,7) at t=20. I have Parcel 1. I need to deliver it to (5,8).
Path: (4,7) -> (4,8) -> (5,8).
Let's trace the next 5 moves (t=20 to t=25):
1. t=20->21: S to (4,8).
   Guard Q at t=21 (3 mod 6) is (2,7). Safe.
   At (4,8), I am on 'y' (Parcel 2 delivery), but I don't have Parcel 2 yet.
2. t=21->22: E to (5,8).
   Guard Q at t=22 (4 mod 6) is (1,7). Safe.
   At (5,8), I am on 'x'. I have Parcel 1. DELIVERED Parcel 1.
   The game ends immediately if I deliver the last parcel, but I still have Parcel 2 to pick up and deliver.
   Wait, the rule says: "If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately".
   I have not delivered the last parcel yet (Parcel 2 is still at (2,1)).
   So the game continues.
3. t=22->23: I need to go back to pick up Parcel 2 at (2,1).
   Path from (5,8) to (2,1):
   (5,8) -> (4,8) -> (3,8) -> (2,8) -> (2,7) -> (2,6) -> (2,5) -> (2,4) is wall.
   Wait, (2,4) is a wall.
   Path: (5,8) -> (4,8) -> (3,8) -> (2,8) -> (1,8) -> (1,7) -> (1,6) -> (1,5) -> (2,5) -> (3,5) -> (3,4) -> (3,3) -> (2,3) -> (2,2) -> (2,1).
   This is long.
   Let's re-evaluate the path from (5,8) to (2,1).
   (5,8) -> (4,8) -> (3,8) -> (2,8) -> (1,8) -> (1,7) -> (1,6) -> (1,5) -> (2,5) -> (3,5) -> (3,4) -> (3,3) -> (2,3) -> (2,2) -> (2,1).
